regime_fall.current_status: A fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran entered its fourth day on April 11, 2026, but is rapidly deteriorating amid escalating tensions: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz on April 10 in response to Israeli strikes in Lebanon, Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum to reopen it or face power plant destruction, and high-stakes US-Iran negotiations led by VP Vance began in Islamabad. The IRGC remains in de facto control with the new Supreme Leader issuing rare public statements, while internal paranoia and surveillance have intensified across Iranian provinces.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum on April 10, 2026 for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face obliteration of power plants, coinciding with Iran's closure of the strait in response to Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon and the start of high-stakes US-Iran negotiations in Islamabad.
